page: Microsoft Azure B2ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. This configuration features 2 physical cores, 2 vCPUs, and 1.0 thread per core, operating on the arm64 architecture. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and gzip compression single-threaded tests scoring in the average range. However, multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests, such as read-only small block at 140.71 GB/sec and write large block at 16.97 GB/sec, fall into the poor and weak categories respectively. Passmark CPU and memory scores are also poor, reflecting the burstable, budget-oriented nature of the hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B2ps_v2 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that do not require continuous full CPU utilization. The burstable CPU allocation model allows users to pay for a lower baseline performance while retaining the ability to handle occasional spikes. Due to its weak multi-core performance, poor memory bandwidth, and lack of local storage, this server is not suitable for high-performance computing, database operations, or large-scale LLM inference. Instead, it is best suited for lightweight general-purpose tasks, development and testing environments, low-traffic web serving, and basic background automation.
description: Microsoft Azure B2ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz. Operating on the arm64 architecture, it includes 8.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and lacks local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate average single-core performance, but weak multi-core and poor memory bandwidth results, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3597.88 and a Passmark memory score of 874.38. This burstable allocation model provides a cost-effective solution for workloads with variable CPU demands. The server is best suited for low-traffic web serving, lightweight general-purpose tasks, and development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2ps_v2 is a burstable arm64 virtual machine featuring an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, designed for lightweight workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2ps_v2 is a burstable arm64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable ARM-based virtual machine designed for lightweight development environments and low-intensity general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Features 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 8.0 GB of system memory
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost-efficient variable workloads
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage options
Best suited for development, testing, and low-traffic web serving
